Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets Cloud Instance Windows Registry Power Shell Interpreter Network Traffic File System Target Artifact (Tasks) Credential Store Application Log User Account Accessibility Alternate Channel
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Email Delivery Method Artificial Intelligence Reg.exe Utility PowerShell Script Process Creation (Mechanism) Windows Registry Key Creation (Mechanism) Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder User Account Authentication (Mechanism) Keylogging Mimikatz Tool Security Software Discovery Network Traffic Flow (Mechanism) File and Directory Discovery Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol Exfiltration Over Other Network Medium Network Traffic Content (Mechanism) File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker uses artificial intelligence to gather information about healthcare and public health organizations.

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker identifies vulnerabilities in the network infrastructure by monitoring network traffic between nodes.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: The attacker creates custom malware to bypass security measures by utilizing cloud instances.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2024-11680 (Suggested): ProjectSend contains an improper authentication vulnerability that allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to enable unauthorized modification of the application's configuration via crafted HTTP requests to options.php.
stage.description: The attacker targets specific employees with tailored phishing emails to gain initial access.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-25280 (Suggested): D-Link DIR-820 routers contain an OS command injection vulnerability that allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to escalate privileges to root via a crafted payload with the ping_addr parameter to ping.ccp.
stage.description: The attacker exploits vulnerabilities in websites visited by employees to gain initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2010-1297 (Suggested): Adobe Flash Player contains a memory corruption vulnerability that allows remote attackers to execute code or cause denial-of-service (DoS).
stage.capec: CAPEC-108: Command Line Execution through SQL Injection
stage.description: The attacker uses PowerShell to execute malicious scripts.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-47565 (Suggested): QNAP VioStar NVR contains an OS command injection vulnerability that allows authenticated users to execute commands via a network.
stage.description: The attacker utilizes WMIC to execute system commands.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: The attacker schedules tasks to maintain persistence.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.technique_name: T1060: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
stage.capec: CAPEC-270: Modification of Registry Run Keys
stage.description: The attacker modifies registry keys for persistence.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker elevates privileges to gain control.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: The attacker exploits vulnerabilities in public-facing applications.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.capec: CAPEC-191: Read Sensitive Constants Within an Executable
stage.description: The attacker obfuscates code to evade detection.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: The attacker executes malicious binaries through system proxy.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker attempts brute force login attempts.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.description: The attacker captures credentials using keyloggers.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.capec: CAPEC-226: Session Credential Falsification through Manipulation
stage.description: The attacker dumps system credentials.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: The attacker discovers installed security software.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.description: The attacker moves laterally through remote services.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-645: Use of Captured Tickets (Pass The Ticket)
stage.description: The attacker uses valid accounts to move laterally.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker collects data from local systems.

stage.name: Collection
stage.description: The attacker discovers files and directories.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates data over alternative protocols.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.technique_name: T1438: Exfiltration Over Other Network Medium
stage.description: The attacker exfiltrates data over other network mediums.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker disrupts healthcare operations.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker destroys sensitive data.

Scenario.summary: Operation Dust Storm is a complex cyber attack campaign targeting healthcare and public health organizations. The attackers use various techniques to gather information, gain initial access, persist, elevate privileges, evade defense mechanisms, access credentials, move laterally, collect data, and finally impact the organization by disrupting operations or destroying sensitive data.
